signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% YoY (vs. 2.0% Prior, 2.4% Forecast)** | YES | HIGH confidence[1]

This data, released around early April 2026, shows inflation rising modestly but undershooting expectations amid global energy volatility from the Iran/Middle East conflict, potentially impacting Bank of Korea rate cut bets as it held rates at 2.5% on April 10[1][3].[3]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Bosnia-Herzegovina confirmed as Switzerland's Group B opponent for 2026 FIFA World Cup** | **YES** | **HIGH** confidence
signals.raw:
Bosnia-Herzegovina confirmed as Switzerland's Group B opponent for 2026 FIFA World Cup** | **YES** | **HIGH** confidence[1][4]

The UEFA Playoff A qualifier has been resolved with Bosnia-Herzegovina advancing to face Switzerland, Canada, and Qatar in Group B, completing the group draw announced earlier[1][4]. This resolution, reported today, finalizes all 12 groups for the June 11–July 19, 2026 to
matched_markets: Will Switzerland win Group B in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

signals.headline: Ethereum ETFs Record 4th Straight Day of Inflows on April 14, Totaling $212M Streak Amid ETH Price Push to $2,400** | YE
signals.raw:
Ethereum ETFs Record 4th Straight Day of Inflows on April 14, Totaling $212M Streak Amid ETH Price Push to $2,400** | YES | HIGH[1][3]

U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs saw $53M net inflows on April 14, led by Fidelity ($38M) and BlackRock ($10.49M), with no outflows across funds, marking the fourth consecutive inflow day and reversing prior negative trends[1]. Ethereum tested $2,400 resistance for the fir
matched_markets: Will the price of Ethereum be between $2,300 and $2,400 on April 20?

signals.headline: No New April-Specific Resolutions for Ethereum Beyond ETF Data** | NO | MED[1][2]
signals.raw:
No New April-Specific Resolutions for Ethereum Beyond ETF Data** | NO | MED[1][2]

April ETF inflows reached $171M after months of $2.8B outflows, with Bitmine expanding ETH holdings to 4.87M tokens (3M staked), but no major regulatory or upgrade confirmations reported[1][2]. Older recaps note ETH rebound to $1,650 by May 1 (2025 context), not right-now breaking news[2].
matched_markets: Will the price of Ethereum be between $2,300 and $2,400 on April 20?
